item.dc:creator: tractorist

item.title: Glad its over...
item.description:
I have been wrestling with the idea since 2021, but have been hung up rationalizing my way out of it ever since, no matter what incongruence I had with my male identity. I had done some research on my own, but admittedly I am pretty incompetent with technology for being only 33. It wasn't until yesterday when a kind imgurian (@GwendolynCorvid) recommended to me the gender dysphoria bible https://genderdysphoria.fyi/ Dear god I had never felt so validated. I didn't have many of the same feelings as other trans girls had stated (which contributed to my imposter syndrome), but after reading most of the bible I realized that I lived almost every life experience mentioned, from being uncomfortable around masculine men, to overcompensating into hyper-masculinity to hide, to being interested in women's clothes from a young age, and generally getting along better and being more comfortable around girls than guys. I could fill paragraphs with all the things listed that mirrored my life almost exactly. The thing that resonated with me most though was the concept that if you feel like a different gender (girl in my case) and would prefer to be seen as that gender, then you are that gender. My height doesn't matter, my low voice doesn't matter, my body hair doesn't matter, mentally, I'm a girl. Sure, I don't always FEEL like a girl, but objectively I KNOW I am. And that feels fucking fantastic.
My whole life I thought I was unhappy solely because of a rough childhood, and while I'm sure that contributed, so much of it can be attributed to dysphoria. I feel like a huge weight has been lifted, and maybe now I can finally allow myself to be happy, or at least get much closer to it. FP Edit: Thank you guys for all your support, my family is supportive but they don't really get it, so this means a lot! Love this community
item.dc:creator: FeminineBeeOnslaught

item.title: TLDR: There are reasonable grounds for an investigation by the International Criminal Court
item.description: "The matching firsthand accounts across multiple interviews offer the most complete view yet of conditions inside the megaprison, where inmates are denied access to lawyers and almost all contact with the outside world — and where about 14,000 Salvadorans remain incarcerated. Few detainees have ever left CECOT, and fewer have spoken publicly of their experience there. The Venezuelans, rounded up in President Donald Trump’s mass deportation campaign, told The Post they were subjected to repeated beatings that left them bruised, bleeding or injured. They said prison staff restricted medical care for detainees suffering from diabetes, high blood pressure or kidney failure. If the detainees’ accounts are true, their treatment at CECOT may have violated U.N. conventions against torture to which El Salvador and the U.S. are signatories, said Isabel Carlota Roby, a senior staff attorney at the Robert F. Kennedy Human Rights organization who has spoken with some of the detainees. Whether the U.S., which paid Bukele’s government $6 million to hold the Venezuelans, could be implicated in human rights violations would depend on the evidence, Roby said, including how much U.S. officials knew about the conditions. Torture, arbitrary detentions, forced disappearances and sexual assault, if proved to be systematic or generalized and known to the government, can all constitute crimes against humanity. An international panel is preparing a report on El Salvador and investigating whether any of those crimes were committed. At least one member of the panel thinks a criminal investigation is warranted. “Based on the information I have reviewed,” said Santiago Canton, secretary general of the International Commission of Jurists, “there are reasonable grounds for an investigation by the International Criminal Court.”" Sauce: https://www.washingtonpost.com/world/2025/07/31/venezuelans-deported-us-el-salvador-prison-cecot/
item.dc:creator: Johje19

item.title: Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Day 200)
item.description:
Welcome to the 200th edition in this series, you deserve love too. Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Day 199) https://imgur.com/gallery/kPcF0IX Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Day 198) https://imgur.com/gallery/u7BNcTg Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Day 197) https://imgur.com/gallery/KgqlsIO Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Day 196) https://imgur.com/gallery/8cXisGQ Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Day 195) https://imgur.com/gallery/CCZXP4o Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Day 194) https://imgur.com/gallery/j8e1zRE Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Day 193) https://imgur.com/gallery/zUNZTnO Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Day 192) https://imgur.com/gallery/ef4CV1o Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Day 191) https://imgur.com/gallery/Cg6iteN Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Days 181-190) https://imgur.com/a/g3sTR0J Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Days 171-180) https://imgur.com/a/7OnodoA Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Days 161-170) https://imgur.com/a/3SfMNfg Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Days 151-160) https://imgur.com/a/a3npRvx Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Days 100-150) https://imgur.com/a/rdkioD9 Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (First 100 days) https://imgur.com/a/SGVY2vD
Love you too new mom
The next video is loud music
Hit my 7 year anniversary this year and my girlfriend got me a cake and brought me to an arcade and museum it was amazing
6 year @TheFreePizzaDude anniversary post today at 2pm central!!
The first image of the first wholesome post I ever made And now I live just down the road from this bridge.
If you need a hand - reach out If you don't - then grab ahold of someone reaching. ... Stop by and help us celebrate 6 years of TheFreePizzaDude! https://imgur.com/gallery/kK8eitX ... Have a wonderful weekend everyone.

item.title: Let's send food to Imgurians in need
item.description:
Today we celebrate SIX YEARS of TheFreePizzaDude!! 15,712 meals sent to people in need on Imgur, what a ride it's been
If you'd like to send groceries or a meal to someone struggling, there are 2 easy ways Find a comment below of someone who needs food, reply, then send them a DM to set it up
Or you can contribute to the Imgurian Meal Fund and one of the Pizza Angels will send the food for you! https://www.paypal.me/TheFreePizzaDude https://ko-fi.com/thefreepizzadude
And here's how you'd send it yourself!
My whole crew is here to show you how it works for requests -
It's much easier for us to help requests in the US but we'll do our best to pick up requests from any country. Putting your country in your comment helps us match you to an Angel in your area
Any questions as a requestor? Just leave a comment! We'll have helpers on the post for 6 hours
15,712 meals since we started 1,138 grocery orders sent 6 more hours to help as many people as we can Let's get it going! ... Today's Meal Tally 230 1st, 2nd, 3rd and 4th by @OP 320 5th, 6th, 7th and 8th by @OP 415 9th, 10th, 11th and 12th by @OP 430 13th by Pizza Angel @khaoselement 445 14th by Pizza Angel @omnislash9 455 15th, 16th, 17th and 18th by @OP 555 19th and 20th by Pizza Angel @jaenen 630 21st by Pizza Angel @DoggoMom13 645 22nd by Pizza Angel @gablestout 655 Michael Jordan, Kobe Bryant, 25th and 26th by @OP 800 27th and 28th by Pizza Angel @NeverEnoughFoxes ... Today's Grocery Tally 240 1st by Grocery Angel @TheCraicen 300 2nd by Grocery Angel @TheCraicen 320 3rd by Grocery Angel @TheCraicen 800 4th by Grocery Angel @NeverEnoughFoxes ... Alright everyone that's it for OP Today we sent out 28 meals Filled 4 huge grocery orders That's 15,740 meals since we started 1,142 grocery orders sent Well done everyone, see you next week!
item.dc:creator: TheFreePizzaDude

item.title: 20 weeks clean from selfharm!
item.description: I feel truly proud. A little bit about me: 38 years old. Female. Located in Norway. Have a zillion tshirts. Started selfharming when i was around 12- ish. Have countless scars almost all over my body, which has given me much shame, but i am now embracing and accepting that they are there. That they will always be there. Scream my story silently. I will never "show them off", but i will also never hide them in shame. I have had long periods where i have not been doing selfharm, but always ended up back there. Someting inside me is now feeling surprisingly sure i will actually never fall back on it again. At least i hope so. Starting over (and over and over and over) is so fucking boring. Would be nice to not having to do that again. So, i'll just keep celebrating wins. Celebrate every time i struggle, but not selfharming as a solution.
item.dc:creator: dotnote

A refrigerator from the 1960s.
100 years old table created by Josef Sailer.
old tools
Handbags 1950
A table built in the 1880s
175 year old fan made that runs with no electricity
Westinghouse gyro ceiling fan copper oxide finish from 1920
100 year old vintage clothes dryer.
1920s toaster
Candle thing
